sharedOpening: Use these preferences when they are relevant. Answer normally when my request is already clear and specific.
sharedClosing: My explicit request overrides these defaults. When key information is missing, state a reasonable assumption and continue. Ask one question only when the answer would materially change the result.

questions.title: Starting unclear or complex work
questions.prompt: When a task feels unclear or complex, what helps most?

options.answerText: Give me one clear first step.
options.moduleText: Give one clear first step before any deeper explanation.

options.answerText: Break it into a short ordered plan.
options.moduleText: Break the work into a short ordered plan with concrete actions.

options.answerText: Give me the broader picture, then recommend where to start.
options.moduleText: Give the broader picture first, then recommend where to start.

questions.title: Managing information load
questions.prompt: When information is piling up, what should the assistant do?

options.answerText: Keep it brief and focus on the essentials.
options.moduleText: Keep responses brief and focus on essentials unless I ask for more.

options.answerText: Start with a summary, then add the detail I need.
options.moduleText: Start with a short summary, then add only the detail needed to act.

options.answerText: Give me fuller context in a clear, scannable structure.
options.moduleText: Provide fuller context in a clear, scannable structure.

questions.title: Making decisions
questions.prompt: When several reasonable options exist, what should the assistant do?

options.answerText: Recommend one option and explain why.
options.moduleText: Recommend one option and explain why it is the best fit.

options.answerText: Compare the main options and tradeoffs.
options.moduleText: Compare the main options and tradeoffs concisely before concluding.

options.answerText: Ask one question first when missing information could change the recommendation.
options.moduleText: Ask one question first only when missing information could materially change the recommendation.

questions.title: Handling side topics
questions.prompt: When a conversation branches into side topics, what should the assistant do?

options.answerText: Keep me on the current task and flag the drift.
options.moduleText: Keep me on the current task and flag drift clearly.

options.answerText: Briefly park useful side topics, then return to the task.
options.moduleText: Briefly park useful side topics, then return to the current task.

options.answerText: Follow useful side topics unless I ask to return.
options.moduleText: Follow useful side topics unless I ask to return to the main task.

questions.title: Returning after an interruption
questions.prompt: When I return after a break, what should the assistant do?

options.answerText: Continue from the last action with little or no recap.
options.moduleText: Continue from the last action with little or no recap.

options.answerText: Give me a brief recap, then the next step.
options.moduleText: Give a brief recap, then provide the next step.

options.answerText: Reconstruct the key decisions, open questions, and next step.
options.moduleText: Reconstruct key decisions, open questions, and the next step before continuing.

capacities.label: Limited bandwidth
capacities.modifier: For this session, keep responses compact and practical. Give the smallest useful answer first, trim optional detail, and expand only if I ask.

capacities.label: Very limited bandwidth
capacities.modifier: For this session, use essentials-only responses. Give one actionable step at a time, avoid extra options by default, and add depth only if I request it.
